mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, labor market weakness signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized demand growth that boosts corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52% over consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns specifically during recovery periods, or the mechanism shows negative alpha when applied to post-2008 recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.25 percent signals restrictive monetary conditions that increase discount rates and compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, or the threshold effect vanishes in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease fails to generate positive 60-day returns, with the interaction term showing zero or negative predictive accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal renewed construction activity and upstream supply chain demand, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that trigger profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops below 95.0 during late-cycle phases, or the threshold loses statistical significance in a regime-filtered backtest.
